meaning: n.煮布锅(漂白,染色用),染布缸
examples: The kier is loaded with cloth in rope form and the scouring chemicals are pumped through the cloth.
In the 1850's Samuel M. Kier, a manufacturer in western Pennsylvania, began collecting the oil from local seepages and refining it into kerosene.
Norfolk and Suffolk police have been singled out by director of public prosecutions Kier Starmer QC.
Developers Kier will submit a revised planning application in the spring, which will go out to consultation.
